data.title: Big River Steel, BRS Finance, and BRS Intermediate offer cash tender offers and consent solicitations. U.S. Steel is involved in the process.
data.description: Pittsburgh-based U.S. Steel Corporation revealed plans for its subsidiaries, including Big River Steel LLC, BRS Finance Corp., and BRS Intermediate Holdings LLC, to start cash tender offers. These offers, collectively known as the "Tender Offers," will involve buying a certain amount of undisclosed assets. The subsidiaries of U. S. Steel have officially initiated this process.

data.title: U of Phoenix CIO discusses tech in online education for adults on podcast "The Rant." Jamie Smith highlights AI, student innovation.
data.description: In a recent interview on "The Rant," Jamie Smith, the Chief Information Officer at the University of Phoenix, delved into the topics of artificial intelligence incorporation, student-centric innovation, and the trajectory of career-focused online education. The conversation, which took place in Phoenix on February 20, 2026, showcased Smith's insights on reshaping educational paradigms. The University of Phoenix's approach to redefining education was a focal point, with Smith emphasizing the pivotal role of AI integration in enhancing learning experiences. Discussions also revolved around the institution's commitment to fostering student-centered innovation, tailored to meet the demands of evolving job markets. Smith elaborated on the future outlook for career-aligned online education, highlighting the significance of adaptability and responsiveness in preparing students for professional success in a dynamic landscape.

data.title: Tech stocks surge, Nasdaq 100 surpasses 25,000; Cadence Design Systems Inc. jumps 10%; Palantir surges 4.5%; Palo Alto Networks falls 6%.
data.description: Tech stocks made a strong recovery on Wednesday, with the Nasdaq 100 surging 1.4% to surpass the 25,000 mark. This outpaced the S&P 500's 1% gain and the Dow's 0.7% advance. Cadence Design Systems Inc. (NASDAQ: CDNS) led the tech rally, rising nearly 10% after exceeding fourth-quarter revenue expectations and projecting robust growth for 2026. This was supported by share buybacks and AI-driven demand. The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (NYSE: IGV) also climbed 1.8%, while Palantir Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ: PLTR) surged 4.5%. However, cybersecurity firm Palo Alto Networks Inc. (NASDAQ: PANW) experienced a 6% decline after lowering its annual profit outlook, despite beating quarterly expectations. Share prices dropped to April 8, 2025 levels, coinciding with the Trump tariff shock peak.

data.title: Sam Bankman-Fried refutes FTX collapse as fraud, claims liquidity crisis. FTX repaying customers 119%-143% of claims. Legal fees around $1B drained estate.
data.description: Sam Bankman-Fried is disputing what he views as common misunderstandings surrounding FTX's downfall, stating that the exchange encountered a liquidity crisis rather than insolvency or fraudulent activities. Bankman-Fried defended FTX's financial state at the time of filing for bankruptcy, asserting that customer funds were not lost permanently. He mentioned that the company is currently reimbursing customers at rates ranging from 119% to 143% of their claims. Bankman-Fried suggested that FTX had the capacity to reimburse customers in assets but was forced into bankruptcy prematurely. He revealed that approximately $1 billion in legal fees have been incurred, depleting the company's assets as attorneys dismantled FTX and supervised the reimbursement process.

data.title: Inventor from London, ON invents the BEST VIEW screen protector/magnifier combo, improving phone screen viewing.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on February 20, 2026, an inventor from London, Ontario, Canada, shared his inspiration for creating the BEST VIEW, which combines a magnifier with a screen protector in one innovative product. The inventor believed in the need for a more convenient solution and designed the BEST VIEW to provide users with an improved viewing experience on their phone screens. This product aims to offer enhanced visibility and functionality in a single, user-friendly device.

data.title: Liquid Networx offers dedicated Fortinet engineers for enterprise clients, as a Fortinet Engage Preferred Services Partner.
data.description: San Antonio-based Liquid Networx, a national Fortinet partner, has become the inaugural Fortinet Engage Preferred Services Partner (EPSP) in North America. They are introducing a new service providing dedicated and certified Fortinet engineers for enterprise customers. This service offers specialized assistance in designing, implementing, and providing continuous support for Fortinet solutions like SASE, Zero Trust, and the Fortinet Security Fabric. The EPSP program recognizes partners with proven capabilities and expertise in service delivery.
